title: centranthus calcitrapae subsp. gutierrezii
intro: Centranthus calcitrapae subsp. gutierrezii, also known as Spanish valerian, is a plant species in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae), native to the Iberian Peninsula and southwestern France. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic and sedative effects, which can help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. The health benefits of Centranthus calcitrapae subsp. gutierrezii include its ability to alleviate insomnia, restlessness, and nervousness, making it a popular herbal remedy for stress relief. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote a good night's sleep and improve overall well-being.
